<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The weighted average fair value of options granted during fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100403" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100404" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100405" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> was <em data-td-fact-id="34100406" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$22.07,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100407" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$18.21,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100408" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$18.50</em> respectively. The total intrinsic value of options exercised during fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100409" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100410" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100411" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> were <em data-td-fact-id="34100412" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$10.6</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="34100413" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.3</em> million, and <em data-td-fact-id="34100414" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.4</em> million respectively. The total fair value of options vested during fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100415" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100416" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100417" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em> were <em data-td-fact-id="34100418" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$8.8</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="34100419" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$5.0</em> million, and <em data-td-fact-id="34100420" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.0</em> million respectively.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Restricted common stock activity under the Plans for the <em data-td-fact-id="34100421" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em>consists of the following (units in thousands):</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-taggable-table="table" data-td-v2v-id="1533998759275" id="thunderdome-statement-982888">
<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="finTable" style="width: 95%; text-indent: 0px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-right: 5%;">

		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom;">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".lead.D2" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td colspan="2" id=".amt.D2" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;">
			<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><i>Number of </i></i></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The total fair value of restricted shares that vested was <em data-td-fact-id="34100422" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.7</em> million for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100423" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100424" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.4</em> million for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100425" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100426" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.4</em> million for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100427" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em></p>

</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The total fair value of restricted stock units that vested was <em data-td-fact-id="34100429" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.6</em> million for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100430" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100431" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.9</em> million for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100432" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100433" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.5</em> million for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100434" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016.</em> The restricted stock units vest over a <em data-td-fact-id="34100435" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em>-year period.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Stock-based compensation cost of <em data-td-fact-id="34100436" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$28.2</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="34100437" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$14.6</em> million, and <em data-td-fact-id="34100438" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$9.4</em> million was included in selling, general and administrative expense in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100439" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100440" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100441" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> respectively. The income tax benefit associated with stock-based compensation costs was <em data-td-fact-id="34100442" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.5</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="34100443" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.6</em> million in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100444" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100445" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> respectively. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em>there was <em data-td-fact-id="34100446" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$26.7</em> million of unrecognized compensation cost related to non-vested stock options, non-vested restricted stock units and non-vested restricted stock which will be expensed in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100447" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> through <em data-td-fact-id="34100448" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2022</em> using a <em data-td-fact-id="34100449" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3%</em> forfeiture rate. The weighted average period over which the compensation cost is expected to be recognized is <em data-td-fact-id="34100450" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.1</em> years.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Employee stock purchase plan:&nbsp;</i>In fiscal year <em data-td-fact-id="34100451" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015,</em> the Company established the Bio-Techne Corporation <em data-td-fact-id="34100452" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em> Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), which was approved by the Company&#39;s shareholders on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> October 30, 2014, </em>and which is designed to comply with IRS provisions governing employee stock purchase plans. <em data-td-fact-id="34100453" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">200,000</em> shares were allocated to the ESPP. The Company recorded expense of <em data-td-fact-id="34100454" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$281,000,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100455" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$213,000</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100456" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$144,000</em> expense for the ESPP in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100457" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100458" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100459" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> respectively.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Profit sharing and savings plans:</i><i> </i>The Company has profit sharing and savings plans for its U.S. employees, which conform to IRS provisions for <em data-td-fact-id="34100460" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">401</em>(k) plans. The Company makes matching contributions to the Plan. The Company has recorded an expense for contributions to the plans of <em data-td-fact-id="34100461" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.5</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="34100462" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.2</em> million,&nbsp;and <em data-td-fact-id="34100463" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.2</em> million&nbsp;for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018,&nbsp;</em><em data-td-fact-id="34100464" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100465" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> respectively. The Company operates defined contribution pension plans for its U.K. employees. The Company has recorded an expense for contributions to the plans of <em data-td-fact-id="34100466" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$1.4</em> million, <em data-td-fact-id="34100467" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.8,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100468" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.8</em> million for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em><em data-td-fact-id="34100469" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100470" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> respectively.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Performance incentive programs:</i><i> </i>In fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100471" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> under certain employment agreements and a Management Incentive Plan available to executive officers and certain management personnel, the Company recorded cash bonuses of <em data-td-fact-id="34100472" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$7.2</em> million, granted options for&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="34100473" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">553,750</em> shares of common stock, issued <em data-td-fact-id="34100474" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">14,194</em> restricted common shares and <em data-td-fact-id="34100475" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35,174</em> restricted stock units. In fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100476" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> and fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100477" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016,</em> the Company recorded cash bonuses of <em data-td-fact-id="34100478" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.7</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="34100479" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.2</em> million, granted options for <em data-td-fact-id="34100480" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">896,778</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100481" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">620,917</em> shares of common stock, and issued <em data-td-fact-id="34100482" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">16,653</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100483" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">11,522</em> restricted common stock shares and <em data-td-fact-id="34100484" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">39,931</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100485" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">26,583</em> restricted stock, respectively.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

			<td id=".symb.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 12%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099742" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(2,981</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.3" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 1px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">

			<td id=".amt.4" style="width: 206px; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099743" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(3,815</em></td>

		</tr>

			<td id=".amt.4" style="width: 206px; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099746" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(2,337</em></td>

		</tr>

			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 12%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099747" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(198</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 3px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">)</td>

			<td id=".amt.4" style="width: 206px; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099749" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">43,005</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.4" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 3px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>

</table>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> December 22, 2017, </em>the U.S. government enacted comprehensive tax legislation commonly referred to as the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (the &ldquo;Tax Act&rdquo;). The Tax Act makes broad and complex changes to the U.S. tax code which impacted our fiscal year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018.&nbsp; These impacts include, but are&nbsp;</em><em data-td-fact-id="34100487" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> limited to, (<em data-td-fact-id="34100488" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>) reducing the U.S. federal corporate tax rate, (<em data-td-fact-id="34100489" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>) requiring a <em data-td-fact-id="34100490" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>-time transition tax on certain unrepatriated earnings of foreign subsidiaries that <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> may </em>electively be paid over <em data-td-fact-id="34100491" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">eight</em> years, and (<em data-td-fact-id="34100492" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>) accelerated <em data-td-fact-id="34100493" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> year expensing of certain capital expenditures. The Tax Act reduced the federal corporate tax rate from&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="34100494" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">35%</em>&nbsp;to&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="34100495" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">21%</em>&nbsp;effective <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> January 1, 2018. </em>Internal Revenue Code Section <em data-td-fact-id="34100496" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">15</em> provides that for our fiscal year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018 </em>we calculate a blended corporate tax rate of <em data-td-fact-id="34100497" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">28.1%,</em> which is based on a proration of the applicable tax rates before and after effective date of the Tax Act. The statutory tax rate of&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="34100498" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">21%</em>&nbsp;will apply for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100499" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> and beyond.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Tax Act also puts in place new tax laws that will impact our taxable income beginning in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100500" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019,</em> which include, but are <em data-td-fact-id="34100501" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> limited to (<em data-td-fact-id="34100502" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>) creating a Base Erosion Anti-abuse Tax (BEAT), which is a new minimum tax, (<em data-td-fact-id="34100503" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2</em>) generally eliminating U.S. federal income taxes on dividends from foreign subsidiaries, (<em data-td-fact-id="34100504" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>) a new provision designed to tax currently global intangible low-taxed income (GILTI), (<em data-td-fact-id="34100505" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">4</em>) a provision that could limit the amount of deductible interest expense, (<em data-td-fact-id="34100506" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">5</em>) the repeal of the domestic production activity deduction, (<em data-td-fact-id="34100507" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6</em>) additional limitations on the deductibility of certain executive compensation, and (<em data-td-fact-id="34100508" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7</em>) limitations on the utilization of foreign tax credits to reduce the U.S. income tax liability.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Shortly after the Tax Act was enacted, the SEC staff issued Staff Accounting Bulletin <em data-td-fact-id="34100509" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100510" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118,</em> Income Tax Accounting Implications of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (SAB <em data-td-fact-id="34100511" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118</em>) which provides guidance on accounting for the Tax Act&rsquo;s impact. SAB <em data-td-fact-id="34100512" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118</em> provides a measurement period, which in <em data-td-fact-id="34100513" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> case should extend beyond <em data-td-fact-id="34100514" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> year from the Tax Act enactment date, during which a company acting in good faith <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> may </em>complete the accounting for the impacts of the Tax Act under ASC Topic <em data-td-fact-id="34100515" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740.</em> In accordance with SAB <em data-td-fact-id="34100516" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118,</em> the Company must reflect the income tax effects of the Tax Act in the reporting period in which the accounting under ASC Topic <em data-td-fact-id="34100517" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740</em> is complete.&nbsp; In March <em data-td-fact-id="34100518" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> the FASB issued ASU <em data-td-fact-id="34100519" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100520" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="34100521" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">05,</em> <i>Amendments to SEC Paragraphs Pursuant to SEC Staff Accounting Bulletin (SAB) <em data-td-fact-id="34100522" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100523" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118</em></i>. which added&nbsp;FASB Codification the guidance provided by the SEC in <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> December 2017.&nbsp; </em></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
<div id="PGNUM" style="width: 100%; text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">59</div>

<hr style="border: currentColor; border-image: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); page-break-after: always; background-color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">
<div id="PGHDR" style="width: 100%; text-align: left;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">To the extent that a company&rsquo;s accounting for certain income tax effects of the Tax Act is incomplete, the Company can determine a reasonable estimate for those effects and record a provisional estimate in the financial statements in the <em data-td-fact-id="34100524" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> reporting period in which a reasonable estimate can be determined. If a Company cannot determine a provisional estimate to be included in the financial statements, the Company should continue to apply ASC <em data-td-fact-id="34100525" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740</em> based on the provisions of the tax laws that were in effect immediately prior to the Tax Act being enacted. If a Company is unable to provide a reasonable estimate of the impacts of the Tax Act in a reporting period, a provisional amount must be recorded in the <em data-td-fact-id="34100526" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">first</em> reporting period in which a reasonable estimate can be determined.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We complied with SAB <em data-td-fact-id="34100527" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em data-td-fact-id="34100528" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">118</em> when preparing our annual consolidated financial statements for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018. </em>Reasonable estimates were used in determining several of the components of the impact of the&nbsp;Tax Act, including our fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100529" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> deferred income tax activity and the amount of post-<em data-td-fact-id="34100530" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1986</em> foreign deferred earnings subject to the repatriation&nbsp;transition tax. We are still analyzing certain aspects of the&nbsp;Tax Act including state tax implications&nbsp;and refining our calculations, which could potentially affect the measurement of our deferred tax balances and the amount of the repatriation toll charge liability, and ultimately cause us to revise our initial estimates in future periods. In addition, changes in interpretations, assumptions and guidance regarding the&nbsp;Tax Act, as well as the potential for technical corrections, could have a material impact on our effective tax rate in future periods.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Transition Tax: The transition tax is a tax on the previously untaxed accumulated and current earnings and profits (E&amp;P) of certain of our foreign subsidiaries as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> December 22, 2017.&nbsp;</em>In order to determine the amount of the Transition Tax, we must determine, in addition to other factors, the amount of post-<em data-td-fact-id="34100531" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1986</em> E&amp;P of the relevant subsidiaries, as well as the amount of non-U.S. income taxes paid on such earnings. E&amp;P is similar to retained earnings of the subsidiary but requires other adjustments to conform to U.S. tax rules. We are able to make a reasonable estimate of the transition tax and recorded a provisional transition tax obligation of&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="34100532" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.3</em> million&nbsp;which the&nbsp;Company expects to elect to pay, net of certain tax credit carryforwards, over <em data-td-fact-id="34100533" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">eight</em> years beginning in fiscal year <em data-td-fact-id="34100534" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019.</em> Of this liability, <em data-td-fact-id="34100535" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.3</em> million is recorded as a current liability with the remaining <em data-td-fact-id="34100536" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.0</em> million classified as a long-term liability within our <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018 </em>balance sheet. We are in the process of analyzing if proposed regulations&nbsp;REG-<em data-td-fact-id="34100537" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">104226</em>-<em data-td-fact-id="34100538" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">18</em> issued on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> August 9, 2018 </em>will have any impact to our current estimate of our transition tax liability. In addition, we have considered state income tax implications and concluded at this time that the impact is immaterial. However, we continue to monitor for additional interpretative guidance including information regarding state income tax implications.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The following is a reconciliation of the federal tax calculated at the statutory rate of <em data-td-fact-id="34100539" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">28.1%</em> to the actual income taxes provided (in&nbsp;thousands):</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The effective tax rate for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018 </em>decreased by <em data-td-fact-id="34100540" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">32.2%</em> compared to the prior year. The decrease in the Company&rsquo;s tax rate for fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100541" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> was due to the impact of discrete items, primarily the net tax benefit of <em data-td-fact-id="34100542" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$33.0</em> million related to the Tax Act discussed above. This net tax benefit consisted of <em data-td-fact-id="34100543" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$36.5</em> million due to the re-measurement of the Company&rsquo;s deferred tax accounts to reflect the U.S. federal corporate tax rate reduction impact to our net deferred tax balances offset by expense for the federal transition tax of <em data-td-fact-id="34100544" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.3</em> million. Also impacting the Company&rsquo;s fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100545" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> effective tax rate was a <em data-td-fact-id="34100546" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$2.2</em> million tax benefit related to stock option exercises offset by a net discrete tax expense of <em data-td-fact-id="34100547" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.2</em> million related to the revaluation of contingent consideration, which is <em data-td-fact-id="34100548" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> a tax deductible expense.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
<div id="PGNUM" style="width: 100%; text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">60</div>

<hr style="border: currentColor; border-image: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); page-break-after: always; background-color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">
<div id="PGHDR" style="width: 100%; text-align: left;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The effective tax rate for the year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2017 </em>increased by <em data-td-fact-id="34100549" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.8%</em> compared to the prior year. The increase was primarily due to unfavorable discrete events in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100550" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em> related to the revaluation of contingent consideration which is <em data-td-fact-id="34100551" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> a tax deductible expense. The Company recognized net expense related to discrete tax items of <em data-td-fact-id="34100552" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.8</em> million in fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100553" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> including <em data-td-fact-id="34100554" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.5</em> million in expense related to the revaluation of contingent consideration which is <em data-td-fact-id="34100555" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> a tax deductible expense.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Deferred taxes on the Consolidated Balance Sheets consisted of the following temporary differences (in thousands):</p>

			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 15%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099807" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(126,638</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 1px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
			<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">)</p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 15%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 1px; border-bottom-style: solid;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099808" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(181,043</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.3" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 1px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
			<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">)</p>
			</td>
		</tr>
		<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(204, 238, 255);">
			<td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">
			<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; margin-left: 18pt;">Net deferred tax liabilities</p>
			</td>
			<td id=".lead.2" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 3px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.2" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.2" style="width: 15%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099809" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(86,293</em></td>
			<td id=".trail.2" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 3px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
			<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">)</p>
			</td>

			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 15%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099810" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">(120,596</em></td>

</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 12.25pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">A deferred tax valuation allowance is required when it is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="34100556" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that all or a portion of deferred tax assets will <em data-td-fact-id="34100557" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be realized. The valuation allowance as of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018 </em>was <em data-td-fact-id="34100558" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.0</em> million, a decrease of <em data-td-fact-id="34100559" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$0.3</em> million from the prior year. The decrease was driven by a decrease in the valuation allowance for the Company&rsquo;s net operating loss and credit carryforwards.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em>the <em data-td-fact-id="34100560" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.0</em> million valuation allowance relates to certain foreign and state tax net operating loss and state credit carryforwards that existed at the date the Company acquired ACD, Novus, ProteinSimple and CyVek as well as immaterial amounts generated after the acquisitions. The Company believes it is more likely than <em data-td-fact-id="34100561" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that these tax carryovers will <em data-td-fact-id="34100562" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be realized.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em>the Company has federal operating loss carryforwards of approximately <em data-td-fact-id="34100563" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$38.4</em> million and state operating loss carryforwards of <em data-td-fact-id="34100564" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$72.8</em> million from its acquisitions of ACD, ProteinSimple and CyVek, which are <em data-td-fact-id="34100565" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> limited under IRC Section <em data-td-fact-id="34100566" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">382.</em>&nbsp;As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em>the Company has foreign net operating loss carryforwards of <em data-td-fact-id="34100567" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.2</em> million.&nbsp;The net operating loss carryforwards expire between fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100568" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100569" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2035.</em> The Company has a deferred tax asset of <em data-td-fact-id="34100570" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$14.0</em> million, net of the valuation allowance discussed above, related to the net operating loss carryovers. As of <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em>the Company has federal and state tax credit carryforwards of <em data-td-fact-id="34100571" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$3.5</em> million and <em data-td-fact-id="34100572" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$4.4</em> million, respectively. The federal tax credit carryforwards expire between <em data-td-fact-id="34100573" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100574" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2035.</em> The majority of the state credit carryforwards have <em data-td-fact-id="34100575" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em> expiry date. The Company has a deferred tax asset of <em data-td-fact-id="34100576" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$6.0</em> million, net of the valuation allowance discussed above, related to the tax credit carryovers.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt 0pt 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company has <em data-td-fact-id="34100577" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> recognized a deferred tax liability for unremitted foreign earnings of approximately <em data-td-fact-id="34100578" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$124.6</em> million from its foreign operations because its subsidiaries have invested or will invest the undistributed earnings indefinitely. The transition tax noted above results in the previously untaxed foreign earnings being included in the federal and state fiscal <em data-td-fact-id="34100579" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> taxable income. We are currently analyzing our global working capital requirements and the potential tax liabilities that would be incurred if the non-U.S. subsidiaries distribute cash to the U.S. parent, which include local country withholding tax and potential U.S. state taxation.&nbsp; At this time, and until we fully analyze the applicable provisions of the Tax Act, our intention with respect to unremitted foreign earnings is to continue to indefinitely reinvest outside the U.S. those earnings needed for working capital or additional foreign investment. &nbsp;&nbsp; Therefore, it is <em data-td-fact-id="34100580" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> practical to estimate the amount of the deferred income tax liabilities related to investments in these foreign subsidiaries.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
<div id="PGNUM" style="width: 100%; text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">61</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company does <em data-td-fact-id="34100581" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> believe it is reasonably possible that the total amounts of unrecognized tax benefits will significantly increase in the next <em data-td-fact-id="34100582" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">twelve</em> months. The Company files income tax returns in the U.S federal and certain state tax jurisdictions, and several jurisdictions outside the U.S. The Company&#39;s federal returns are subject to tax assessment for <em data-td-fact-id="34100583" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2015</em> and subsequent years. State and foreign income tax returns are generally subject to examination for a period of <em data-td-fact-id="34100584" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">three</em> to <em data-td-fact-id="34100585" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">five</em> years after filing of the respective return. The state impact of any federal changes remains subject to examination by various states for a period of up to <em data-td-fact-id="34100586" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> year after formal notification to the states.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>&nbsp;</b></i></p>
</div>


<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="29169" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1533998782933" id="thunderdome-statement-982959">
<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note <em data-td-fact-id="34100587" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">11.</em> Segment Information:</b></i></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 12.25pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company has <em data-td-fact-id="34100588" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">three</em> reportable segments based on the nature of its products; they are Biotechnology, Protein Platforms and Diagnostics.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company&#39;s Biotechnology segment is comprised of the legacy biotechnology business and ACD. These businesses manufacture consumables used for conducting laboratory experiments by both industry and academic scientists within the biotechnology and biomedical life science fields Our protein, antibodies, immunoassays, and small molecules products in the legacy biotechnology business are tools to help our customers analyze the protein component of cells, and the ACD technology allows our customers to analyze the genetic changes within cells. When used together, our customers have a more complete set of tools to study normal and abnormal cell behavior. <em data-td-fact-id="34100589" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">No</em> customer in the Biotechnology segment accounted for more than <em data-td-fact-id="34100590" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">10%</em> of the segment&rsquo;s net sales for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em><em data-td-fact-id="34100591" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">2017,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100592" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">2016.</em></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company&#39;s Protein Platforms segment develops and commercializes proprietary systems and consumables for protein analysis. <em data-td-fact-id="34100593" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">No</em> customer in the Protein Platforms segment accounted for more than <em data-td-fact-id="34100594" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">10%</em> of the segment&rsquo;s net sales for the years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018, </em><em data-td-fact-id="34100595" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">2017,</em> and <em data-td-fact-id="34100596" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">2016.</em></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company&#39;s Diagnostics reporting segment develops and manufactures a range of controls and calibrators used with diagnostic equipment and as proficiency testing tools, as well as other reagents incorporated into diagnostic kits. One customer accounted for approximately&nbsp;<em data-td-fact-id="34100597" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">12%</em> for the fiscal year ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2017. </em><em data-td-fact-id="34100598" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">No</em> customer in the Diagnostics segment accounted for more than <em data-td-fact-id="34100599" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">10%</em> of the segment&rsquo;s net sales for the fiscal years ended <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> June 30, 2018 </em>and <em data-td-fact-id="34100600" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">2016.</em>&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">There are <em data-td-fact-id="34100601" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">no</em> concentrations of business transacted with a particular customer or supplier or concentrations of revenue from a particular product or geographic area that would severely impact the Company in the near term.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">

			<td id=".lead.3" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 3px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</td>
			<td id=".symb.3" style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;">$</td>
			<td id=".amt.3" style="width: 12%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom-color: rgb(0, 0, 0); border-bottom-width: 3px; border-bottom-style: double;"><em data-td-fact-id="34099875" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">120,584</em></td>

			<td id=".trail.4" nowrap="true" style="width: 1%; padding-bottom: 3px; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&nbsp;</td>
		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company has some integrated facilities that serve multiple segments. As such, asset and capital expenditure information by reportable segment has <em data-td-fact-id="34100602" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">not</em> been provided and is <em data-td-fact-id="34100603" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">not</em> available, since the Company does <em data-td-fact-id="34100604" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">not</em> produce or utilize such information internally. In addition, although depreciation and amortization expense is a component of each reportable segment&rsquo;s operating results, it is <em data-td-fact-id="34100605" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;">not</em> discretely identifiable.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The revenues from external customers within each of our segments represent a similar group of products.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Following is financial information relating to geographic areas (in thousands):</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

		</tr>

</table>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Long-lived assets are comprised of land, buildings and improvements and equipment, net of accumulated depreciation and other assets.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><sup>(<em data-td-fact-id="34100607" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1</em>)</sup> Net earnings in total and per share do <em data-td-fact-id="34100608" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> sum due to rounding</p>

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="9" id="29171" sectdesc="Note to Financial Statements">&nbsp;</div>

<div data-td-parse-id="1" data-td-statement-type="note" data-td-v2v-id="1533998802264" id="thunderdome-statement-982903">
<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><b><i>Note <em data-td-fact-id="34100609" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13.</em> Subsequent Events:</i></b></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&nbsp;</i></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> July 2, 2018, </em>the Company acquired QT Holdings Corporation (Quad) for approximately <em data-td-fact-id="34100610" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$20</em> million plus <em data-td-fact-id="34100611" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$51</em> million in potential milestone payments. On <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> August 1, 2018, </em>the Company acquired Exosome Diagnostics, Inc. (Exosome) for approximately <em data-td-fact-id="34100612" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$250</em> million plus <em data-td-fact-id="34100613" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$325</em> million in potential milestone payments. The purchase accounting for these acquisitions are in progress.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">In connection with the acquisition of Exosome Diagnostics, Inc. on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> August 1, 2018, </em>the Company entered into a new credit facility that provides for a revolving credit facility of <em data-td-fact-id="34100614" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$600</em> million, which can be increased by an additional <em data-td-fact-id="34100615" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$200</em> million subject to certain conditions,&nbsp;and a term loan of <em data-td-fact-id="34100616" style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">$250</em> million.&nbsp; The credit facility is governed by a Credit Agreement dated <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> August 1, 2018 </em>and matures on <em data-td-invalid-detail="1" style="font-style: normal; font-weight: inherit;"> August 1, 2023.</em></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">

<div contenteditable="false" docsect="12" id="29172" sectdesc="Other">&nbsp;</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><b>Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm</b></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">To the Shareholders and Board of Directors<br>
Bio-Techne Corporation:</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Opinion on the Consolidated Financial Statements</i></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We have audited the accompanying consolidated balance sheets of Bio-Techne Corporation and subsidiaries. as of June 30, 2018 and 2017, the related consolidated statements of earnings and comprehensive income, shareholders&rsquo; equity, and cash flows for each of the fiscal years in the three&#8209;year period ended June 30, 2018, and the related notes (collectively, the &ldquo;consolidated financial statements&rdquo;). In our opinion, the consolidated financial statements present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of the Company as of June 30, 2018 and 2017, and the results of its operations and its cash flows for each of the years in the three&#8209;year period ended June 30, 2018, in conformity with U.S.&nbsp;generally accepted accounting principles.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We also have audited, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States), the Company&rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting as of June 30, 2018, based on criteria established in <i>Internal Control &ndash; Integrated Framework (2013)</i> issued by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission, and our report dated August 27, 2018 expressed an unqualified opinion on the effectiveness of the Company&rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Basis for Opinion</i></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">These consolidated financial statements are the responsibility of the Company&rsquo;s management. Our responsibility is to express an opinion on these consolidated financial statements based on our audits. We are a public accounting firm registered with the PCAOB and are required to be independent with respect to the Company in accordance with the U.S.&nbsp;federal securities laws and the applicable rules and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission and the PCAOB.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We conducted our audits in accordance with the standards of the PCAOB. Those standards require that we plan and perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the consolidated financial statements are free of material misstatement, whether due to error or fraud. Our audits included performing procedures to assess the risks of material misstatement of the consolidated financial statements, whether due to error or fraud, and performing procedures that respond to those risks. Such procedures included examining, on a test basis, evidence regarding the amounts and disclosures in the consolidated financial statements. Our audits also included evaluating the accounting principles used and significant estimates made by management, as well as evaluating the overall presentation of the consolidated financial statements. We believe that our audits provide a reasonable basis for our opinion.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="text-indent: 9pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">/s/ KPMG LLP</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We have served as the Company&rsquo;s auditor since 2002.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Minneapolis, Minnesota<br>
August 27, 2018</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
<div id="PGNUM" style="width: 100%; text-align: center; font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt;">65</div>

<hr style="border: currentColor; border-image: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: rgb(0, 0, 0); page-break-after: always; background-color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">
<div id="PGHDR" style="width: 100%; text-align: left;"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></div>
</div>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><b>&nbsp;</b></p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><b>Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm</b></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">To the Shareholders and Board of Directors<br>
Bio-Techne Corporation:</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Opinion on Internal Control Over Financial Reporting </i></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We have audited Bio-Techne Corporation&rsquo;s and subsidiaries&rsquo; (the Company) internal control over financial reporting as of June 30, 2018, based on criteria established in <i>Internal Control &ndash; Integrated Framework (2013)</i> issued by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission<i>.</i> In our opinion, the Company maintained, in all material respects, effective internal control over financial reporting as of June 30, 2018, based on criteria established in <i>Internal Control &ndash; Integrated Framework (2013)</i> issued by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We also have audited, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States) (PCAOB), the consolidated balance sheets of the Company as of June 30, 2018 and 2017, and the related consolidated statements of earnings and comprehensive income, shareholders&rsquo; equity, and cash flows for each of the fiscal years in the three-year period ended June 30, 2018, and related notes (collectively, the consolidated financial statements), and our report dated August 27, 2018 expressed an unqualified opinion on those consolidated financial statements.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company acquired Trevigen, Inc.,&nbsp; Atlanta Biologicals, Inc., and Eurocell Diagnostics SAS during fiscal 2018, and management excluded from its assessment of the effectiveness of the Company&rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting as of June 30, 2018, Trevigen Inc.,&nbsp; Atlanta Biologicals, Inc., and Eurocell Diagnostics SAS &rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting associated with total assets of 5.3% and total revenues of 1.7% included in the consolidated financial statements of the Company as of and for the year ended June 30, 2018. Our audit of internal control over financial reporting of the Company also excluded an evaluation of the internal control over financial reporting of Trevigen, Inc., Atlanta Biologicals, Inc., and Eurocell Diagnostics SAS.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Basis for Opinion </i></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company&rsquo;s management is responsible for maintaining effective internal control over financial reporting and for its assessment of the effectiveness of internal control over financial reporting, included in the accompanying Item 9A Management&rsquo;s Report on Internal Control over Financial Reporting. Our responsibility is to express an opinion on the Company&rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting based on our audit. We are a public accounting firm registered with the PCAOB and are required to be independent with respect to the Company in accordance with the U.S. federal securities laws and the applicable rules and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission and the PCAOB.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">We conducted our audit in accordance with the standards of the PCAOB. Those standards require that we plan and perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about whether effective internal control over financial reporting was maintained in all material respects. Our audit of internal control over financial reporting included obtaining an understanding of internal control over financial reporting, assessing the risk that a material weakness exists, and testing and evaluating the design and operating effectiveness of internal control based on the assessed risk. Our audit also included performing such other procedures as we considered necessary in the circumstances. We believe that our audit provides a reasonable basis for our opinion.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Definition and Limitations of Internal Control Over Financial Reporting </i></p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">A company&rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting is a process designed to provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and the preparation of financial statements for external purposes in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles. A company&rsquo;s internal control over financial reporting includes those policies and procedures that (1)&nbsp;pertain to the maintenance of records that, in reasonable detail, accurately and fairly reflect the transactions and dispositions of the assets of the company; (2)&nbsp;provide reasonable assurance that transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of financial statements in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and that receipts and expenditures of the company are being made only in accordance with authorizations of management and directors of the company; and (3)&nbsp;provide reasonable assurance regarding prevention or timely detection of unauthorized acquisition, use, or disposition of the company&rsquo;s assets that could have a material effect on the financial statements.</p>

<p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Because of its inherent limitations, internal control over financial reporting may not prevent or detect misstatements. Also, projections of any evaluation of effectiveness to future periods are subject to the risk that controls may become inadequate because of changes in conditions, or that the degree of compliance with the policies or procedures may deteriorate.</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">/s/ KPMG LLP</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt 7.5pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Minneapolis, Minnesota<br>
August 27, 2018&nbsp;</p>

<p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<div data-thunderdome-xbrl-exclude="1" id="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-right: 0pt; margin-left: 0pt;">
